Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

vedic source of mrdanga pranam mantra?

Other · asked by user [] · 2007-08-14 · 4 answers
Someone is asking me at mrdanga.wordpress.com.
user [2] · 2007-08-20
Anyone for this?

"Dear Jagannatha Prabhu,
Thank you for the message. Please forward to me when you get the answer, my concern is that with my little knowledge I have noticed that the pranam mantra may need some correction. It has some incorrect composition and some missing letters. In my knowledge I have found the pranam mantra as follow:
Mridanga brahmarupaaya laabanya roso maadhuri,
sahasra-guna samyuktaa mridangaaya namo namah

I hope you will find the correct one. Haribol!
YS"
user [38] · 2007-08-20
Found this version which seems ok, source unknown to me:

mRdangAya brahmo rupAya lavanam rasa mAdhurI |
sarva mRdanga saMyuktaM mRdangAya namo namaH ||
user [78] · 2007-08-20
I got this mantra from my siksa guru (I get lessons on a video)

Namo Jagannatha Sutayo
Namo Mridanga Namaha
Labanga Rasa Madhuri
Sahasra Guna Samyuktang
Namo Mridangaya Namo Namaha
Namo Baladevaya Namo Namaha.

Translation:
I offer my respectful obeisances unto the son of Jagannatha Misra (Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu). I offer my obeisances to the mridanga from which sweet and necterean sound emanates. I offer my obeisances again and again to the mridanga which has thousands of good qualities. And I offer my obeisances unto Lord Baladeva who assumes the form of a mridanga to serve Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

Do you mean this Mishra pr.?
user [2] · 2007-08-20
yes, but asking for Vedic source
